name: Greg Van Borssum
title: Accredited Speaker
content: A bullied, skinny kid too afraid to stand up for himself, Greg used to live in fear. At age 12, he decided to change and spent his newspaper route money on a gym membership and undertook rigorous weight training and the study of martial arts. He then decided to take on two massive challenges, to become the Australian National Bodybuilding Champion and a Karate Master. It was here Greg learned to unravel the warriors code and by applying its principles he revealed how limitless his life could become. In 2019, Greg became an Accredited Speaker by Toastmasters International, making him 1 of 88 speakers globally in over 40 years to receive this honor. Greg’s ability to transfer his mindset into totally new areas of life and master them in relatively short time has had him labelled by many as the MASTER OF REMASTERY. Let Greg show you the steps to master your mindset and remaster your life.

name: Stan Shih
title: Cofounder of the Acer Group
content: Stan Shih is the Chairman of Stan Shih Foundation, Cofounder & Honorary Chairman of the Acer Group. Mr. Shih serves on the boards of Acer, the former boards of TSMC. He is the Chairman of Cloud Gate Culture and Art Foundation and the head of One Song Orchestra Fun Club. Besides, he acts as commissioner of the Reform for Resilience Commission and Director of CAPRI. Time featured Mr. Shih in its article “60 years of Asian Hero’s” for turning Taiwan into a PC manufacturing powerhouse. He is awarded “Taiwan Cultural Collaboration Medal” by the Ministry of Culture of Taiwan in 2021. Mr. Shih earned a Bachelor’s Master and honorary Ph D in Electronics engineering from National Chiao Tung University in Taiwan. He was awarded an Honorary Doctorate of Technology by Hong Kong Polytechnic University, and an Honorary Doctor of International Law from the Thousand School of Global Management.

seminar.topic: From Wangdao Perspective to the Competitiveness of Taiwan
seminar.introduction: “New Wangdao” talks about the leadership thinking of any organization by “creating values, balancing current interest and sustaining future development.” In the perspective of Wangdao, there are six criteria to measure the total value of contributions: “visible, direct and current” values, and “invisible, indirect and future” values, while the later is much more important.
